_source: Ohio DNR Lake Erie Fisheries 2024 Annual Report + Ohio Sea Grant telemetry study
_notes: Catch rate 2024: 0.75 fish/hour — above average, ranked 6th since 2006
210 bass tagged across Lake Erie 2023-2024, tracked via 400+ acoustic receivers through 2027
Most bass maintain home ranges under 5 sq miles; subset make long-distance migrations (one fish 500 miles)
Angling-caught fish move nearly 2x farther than electrofishing-caught fish post-tagging
Eastern basin: bass avoid deep central basin hypoxia, use full water column
Round goby makes up 73-75% of eastern basin smallmouth diet
Post-goby invasion: a 6-inch bass is ~17% heavier than pre-goby equivalent
Ontario FMZ 19 abundance above target (1.00 fish/hr) during 2016-2020
World record smallmouth: 10.15 lbs, October 2022, Lake Erie
2025 CAUTION: ODNR reports REDUCED adult populations in central and east zones due to poor 2022-2023 year-class hatches. Expect fewer fish but larger average size from surviving adults.
East basin historical catch rate target: 1.00 fish/hr (highest of all three basins)
Largemouth bass displacing smallmouth in western basin — less relevant to eastern basin fishing

population_outlook_2025.implication: Fewer numbers but larger fish. Focus on high-probability spots. Don't leave fish to find fish.

pre_spawn_staging.behavior: Moving toward spawning grounds along first major break lines

spawn.behavior: On nests, rock/gravel/sand, 55-65°F trigger. Peaks early-mid June (2-3 wks after western basin)

post_spawn.behavior: Dispersing from spawning areas to offshore rock humps and ledges

summer_deep.behavior: Thermocline drives deeper. Goby-imitating tube jigs / drop-shot. 20-32ft typical, 50+ in August heat

fall_staging.behavior: October peak — fat fish chasing bait before winter. Rocky points in 30-45ft. Most predictable window of the year

winter.behavior: Inactive. Eastern basin fish drop to 35-50ft thermal refuge
